title: EAFIX Registry Post-Merge Deterministic Validation Remediation Plan

repository.revert_policy: Use forward remediation. Revert PR #74 only if the merged registry system breaks unrelated repository functionality or corrupts existing authoritative files.

objective.primary: Repair the deterministic-build, cross-reference, Python compatibility, and CI enforcement defects that remain on master after PR #74.
objective.completion_definition: Every managed generated artifact is deterministic and covered by no-write check mode; all registry validation passes on Python 3.9 and 3.11; two consecutive builds produce no diff; executable GitHub checks pass; no authority cutover occurs.

required_references.role: current canonical module identity and responsibility authority
current canonical process-step and handoff authority
current phase, validation-contract, and failure-contract authority

rules: The merged JSONL registries remain shadow canonical candidates.
Do not change routing instructions, doc_authority.json, supersession state, or canonical authority assignments in this remediation PR.
Existing canonical module and process catalogs continue to win conflicts.
Do not remove the 25 review items or two orphan classifications without separate evidence and governance approval.
If a required reference is missing, record that fact and do not make authority conclusions that depend on it.

explicit_false_positive.finding: Add main to the registry workflow filters
explicit_false_positive.reason: The repository default branch is master and the workflow already targets master and develop.

environmental_blocker.name: GitHub Actions billing/account lock
environmental_blocker.effect: Checks may fail before any job starts.
environmental_blocker.rule: Do not count startup-blocked checks as passing or as code failures. Complete local validation, but do not merge the remediation until CI can execute.

confirmed_defects.title: Manifest paths are platform-dependent
confirmed_defects.required_fix: Use repository-relative POSIX paths with Path.as_posix().

confirmed_defects.title: Check mode compares only registry snapshots
confirmed_defects.required_fix: Render and compare every managed generated artifact in --check mode without writing.

confirmed_defects.title: Module process_step_ids are not foreign-key validated
confirmed_defects.required_fix: Validate every module process_step_id against the process registry.

confirmed_defects.title: Writer is incompatible with declared Python 3.9 support
confirmed_defects.required_fix: Replace Path.write_text(..., newline=...) with Python 3.9-compatible stable file writing.

confirmed_defects.title: Generated parity failure is advisory
confirmed_defects.required_fix: Make structural validation and deterministic parity blocking; keep only governance review findings advisory.

implementation_architecture.pattern: pure render, then compare or write

components.name: render_expected_outputs
components.contract: Return a deterministic mapping of repository-relative POSIX paths to exact UTF-8 bytes for every managed generated artifact.

components.name: compare_expected_outputs
components.contract: Compare every expected artifact against disk, report missing/different outputs, and perform no writes.

components.name: write_expected_outputs
components.contract: Write the exact rendered bytes using UTF-8 and LF newlines through Python 3.9-compatible APIs.

components.name: build_manifest
components.contract: Hash authored registry inputs and all generated outputs except the manifest itself; store only POSIX path keys.

components.name: run
components.contract: Load, validate, render, compare or write, classify blocking errors versus advisory findings, print a deterministic summary, and return an appropriate exit code.

tasks.action: Create a clean branch from current master.
acceptance: Branch contains merge commit 5b28d4ac4d695ea5ac319a344f97ce9a7f32ba1e or a later verified master commit.

tasks.action: Read all required routing and authority references.
evidence: List reviewed files in the PR description.
Confirm no authority-cutover file will be modified.

tasks.action: Capture baseline behavior.
evidence: Whether two normal builds modify registry_build_manifest.json

tasks.action: Implement one render function for all managed outputs.
requirements: Render snapshots, graph, reports, Mermaid, Markdown views, and manifest in memory.
Do not hash the manifest itself.
Use stable sorted JSON with a trailing newline.

tasks.action: Implement Python 3.9-compatible file output.
tasks.required_pattern: Use Path.open('w', encoding='utf-8', newline='\n') or binary writes.
tasks.prohibited_pattern: Do not pass newline to Path.write_text.

tasks.action: Make write and check modes consume the same rendered output mapping.
acceptance: No generated artifact has a separate check-mode logic path.
Write mode writes exactly what check mode expects.

tasks.action: Exclude the manifest from its output_hashes.
Normalize all input_hashes and output_hashes keys with as_posix().
Add an internal manifest verifier.
requirements: Every listed input path exists and matches its recorded hash.
Every listed output path exists and matches its recorded hash.
registry_build_manifest.json is not listed as an output.

tasks.action: Compare every managed generated artifact under --check.
acceptance: --check returns zero only when every managed output is current.
Tampering any managed output returns nonzero.

tasks.action: Separate blocking errors from advisory governance findings.
tasks.rule: --report-only may prevent advisory findings from failing the command, but must not hide blocking errors.

tasks.action: Validate module process_step_ids against process registry IDs.
tasks.error_contract: Include registry name, record_id, field, and invalid target ID.

tasks.action: Audit every schema-declared ID-reference field against validate_refs.
tasks.rule: Add validator coverage only for declared relationships. Do not invent new relationships or populate missing data.

phases.name: Add regression and mutation tests
test_rules: Use temporary directories or patched roots.
Never leave committed files modified after a test.
Tests must pass on Windows and Linux.
Python 3.9 CI execution is the authoritative compatibility proof.

tasks.action: Add a Python matrix.
Make integrity and parity blocking.
tasks.required_change: Remove continue-on-error from the structural validation and --check step.

tasks.action: Keep governance reports advisory and upload them with if: always().
Preserve branch filters master and develop.
tasks.prohibited_change: Do not add main solely because of the incorrect review comment.

recommended_workflow_sequence: Run git diff --exit-code for generated directories

acceptance: First command writes only expected generated changes.
Second command reports errors=0 and changed=0.
No authored JSONL record changes unless separately justified.
No routing or authority files change.

tasks.action: Prove two-build determinism.
acceptance: The second build creates no diff.

tasks.action: Review all generated diffs.

phases.name: Full local validation
mutation_tests: Tamper each output class and prove --check fails without rewriting.
Inject an invalid module process_step_id in a temporary fixture and prove reference validation fails.
Restore clean state and prove all validation passes.

commands: git commit -m "fix(registry): remediate deterministic validation defects"

tasks.action: Open a PR against master.
required_pr_sections: Governance boundary and out-of-scope authority cutover

tasks.action: Document disposition of PR #74 review comments.

dispositions.result: rejected because master is the default branch

tasks.action: Resolve the GitHub Actions billing/account lock.

tasks.rule: Skipped, neutral, startup-blocked, or account-locked checks are not passing evidence.

merge_conditions: All blocking checks execute and pass
No unresolved P1/P2 review thread on remediation code
Two consecutive builds produce no diff
PR description matches the final state

change: Add regression and mutation coverage for every confirmed defect

acceptance_criteria: Schema, ID, reference, and authority errors are zero.
Every managed generated artifact is checked by --check.
The suite passes on Python 3.9 and 3.11.
Two consecutive builds produce no Git diff.
Tampering any managed generated artifact causes --check to fail.
Structural validation and generated parity block CI.
The 25 review items and two orphan items remain visible unless separately resolved.
No routing or authority cutover occurs.
GitHub checks execute after the account lock is removed.
Doc ID Validation and title lint pass.
evidence_required: Explicit confirmation that authority cutover was not performed

fail_closed_conditions: Record count changes without approved source changes
Routing or authority files appear in the diff
Review or orphan items disappear without evidence
response: Revert only the remediation branch changes.
Classify the cause as generator logic, source drift, platform variance, or environmental CI blockage.
Open a focused issue for out-of-scope governance or data conflicts.

must_report: Confirmation that authority cutover was not performed
must_not_claim: CI passed when jobs were blocked before execution
Full determinism without two consecutive byte-stable builds
